replication_study.hypothesis: Participants who receive interpersonal attention (condition = 1) will report a higher summed intimacy score (atot, the reverse-coded six-item scale) than participants who do not receive attention (condition = 0).

data_plan.access_details: Openly provided inside replication_data folder of this project; no restrictions.

qualification.explanation: Dataset was collected independently from the original experiments yet implements the same manipulation (attention vs. no-attention) and the same intimacy items, thereby allowing a direct replication test with a new sample.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Uses identical 6-item intimacy scale (post_registration.json, method → outcome_variable) and the same binary attention manipulation (initial_details.txt, HYPOTHESIS). Variable names a1–a6 and "condition" match those in the supplied original SPSS syntax (analysis ohtsubo.sps).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original paper combined data across five separate studies run 2013–2014 with larger Ns (~526; post_registration.json, data → sample_size). Present dataset is a single online run in 2021 with N=41, and therefore constitutes a new, smaller sample collected under potentially different context (online vs laboratory/classroom).

data_plan.notes: Codebook.xlsx not readable with default loader but variable meanings inferred from SPSS syntax. Columns include demographics (age, sex, race) that are not used but available for robustness checks. No identifiable information present; Likert items stored as integers 1–7.

planned_method.steps: 1) Load dataset from /app/data. 2) Reverse-code item a4 into a4r. 3) Compute intimacy scale atot = a1 + a2 + a3 + a4r + a5 + a6. 4) Exclude rows with missing data on condition or atot. 5) Conduct Welch independent-samples t-test comparing atot between condition==1 (attention) and condition==0 (no attention). 6) Compute Cohen’s d. 7) Save and report statistics.
planned_method.models: Welch independent-samples t-test; effect size Cohen’s d.
planned_method.outcome_variable: atot (sum of six intimacy items with a4 reverse-coded).
planned_method.control_variables: None (random assignment expected to balance demographics).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in means of atot between groups (attention − control).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion on key variables (condition, atot).
planned_method.inference_criteria: p < .05 (two-tailed) with effect direction matching original (attention > no attention).

files.replication_data/replicate_analysis.py: Main executable. Builds intimacy scale, performs t-test, computes effect size, prints and writes results to /app/data/replication_results.txt.

codebase.notes: Script hard-coded to default data path /app/data/ohtsubo data.csv but can be overridden via DATA_FILE environment variable. Requires only standard scientific Python stack.

analysis.instructions: Inside container, run:  python replication_data/replicate_analysis.py  (optionally set DATA_FILE if dataset renamed). Script outputs summary and writes replication_results.txt to /app/data.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction of mean difference, t-statistic magnitude, p-value (< .05), and Cohen’s d compared to original Study 2a result (t=5.91, d=2.20; post_registration.json). Successful replication if effect direction positive and p<.05.
